era: 1990-1997. Heavy Punk. E=8 -- metal context. Riot grrrl adjacent. Grief and rage. Capitol Hill.

theory_nodes.concept: E=8 female-led heavy band: Selene Vigil's voice meets the metal context at its own level. No softening, no conventional 'female vocalist' adaptation. The engine's energy calibration is neutral with respect to gender -- E=8 is E=8.
theory_nodes.description: E=8 without gender modifier: the correct calibration for Seven Year Bitch's heaviest material.

theory_nodes.concept: Grief-as-fuel: Stefanie Sargent (died June 27 1992, bassist) and Kurt Cobain (died April 5 1994, community) as emotional substrate of Viva Zapata! The rage in the heaviest material has a specific source.
theory_nodes.description: The losses that deepen Seven Year Bitch's metal material.

theory_nodes.concept: Riot grrrl adjacent + metal: feminist politics + heavy metal sonics. The feminist punk tradition extends into metal territory. Seven Year Bitch demonstrates the tradition is not bounded at the edge of punk intensity.
theory_nodes.description: The specific synthesis that Seven Year Bitch represents.

cross_references.relationship: E=7, riot grrrl/punk, first entry. The 279-328 arc: punk to metal, E=7 to E=8.
Riot grrrl tradition. Kathleen Hanna. Vigil in the same tradition, extended into heavier territory.
Capitol Hill feminist punk at E=6. Seven Year Bitch at E=8 is the metal end of the same scene.
Kurt Cobain. Capitol Hill community loss 1994. Documented as grief-as-fuel context for Viva Zapata.
E=6. The E=6-6-8 sequence: Truly + Brad + Seven Year Bitch. The sharp energy rise at 328.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=8 + E=8. FIRST CANID VOCALIZATION IN SPS. Combined E=16 -- maximum in the 325-328 sequence. Night predator chorus (winter, forest edge) vs. heavy punk grief and rage. Both entries document aggressive acoustic presence at night.

milestones: SEVEN YEAR BITCH RETURN / METAL E=8
